metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1406
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors investigate whether Htt plays a role in transporting NF-kB from the postsynaptic compartment to the nucleus based on the fact that Htt interacts preferable with the active form of NF-kB. They performed photo activation experiments in primary hippocampal cultures of control and conditional htt knockout mice and observed that when htt was missing the half-life of NF-kB movement was double as compared to control mice. This data was in agreement with the quantification of NF-kB levels in the nucleus fraction prepared from control and knockout mice, which showed significantly lower levels in the absence of htt.. Experimental description: From results:
"nuclear fractions prepared from forebrains of (A) 4-month-old Htt knock-out (KO) and control (CTRL) mice (n 1/4 2 non-sex matched, littermate pairs) and (B) 6-month-old HD knock-in (HD) and wild- type control (WT) mice (n 1/4 2 non-sex matched, littermate pairs). See text for quantification of the immunoblots."
